title: Chiesi buys into Arbor gene editing drug for rare kidney disease
summary: Chiesi Group acquired global rights to ABO-101, an experimental gene editing therapy from Arbor Biotechnologies for primary hyperoxaluria type 1 (PH1), a rare genetic kidney disorder. Under the deal, Chiesi pays up to $115 million in upfront and near-term payments with potential for up to $2 billion more in downstream milestone payments and royalties. ABO-101 is designed as a one-time gene editing treatment that could replace current PH1 therapies requiring frequent injections.
